questions.q: Homi J. Bhabha is widely regarded as the father of which national programme?
questions.explanation: Homi Bhabha is celebrated as the principal architect and 'father' of India's atomic energy and nuclear programme.

questions.q: In which scientific field did Homi Bhabha primarily work?
questions.explanation: Bhabha was a theoretical and nuclear physicist whose work spanned cosmic rays and particle physics.

questions.q: Which research institute did Homi Bhabha found in 1945?
questions.explanation: Bhabha founded the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR) in Mumbai in 1945.

questions.q: A scattering process in particle physics is named after Homi Bhabha. What is it called?
questions.explanation: Bhabha scattering describes the scattering of an electron by a positron, named for his work on the process.

questions.q: India's premier nuclear research centre at Trombay is named in honour of Homi Bhabha. What is it called?
options: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research
questions.explanation: The Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) at Trombay was renamed in his memory after his death.

questions.q: Bhabha scattering specifically involves the interaction of which two particles?
questions.explanation: Bhabha scattering is electron-positron scattering, a fundamental process in quantum electrodynamics.

questions.q: Homi Bhabha was a citizen and native of which country?
questions.explanation: Bhabha was an Indian physicist, born in Bombay (Mumbai) into a prominent Parsi family.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian city was Homi Bhabha born?
questions.explanation: Homi Jehangir Bhabha was born in Bombay, now Mumbai, in 1909.

questions.q: Bhabha is best known for establishing institutions in which broad area of science and technology?
questions.explanation: He built India's institutions for atomic energy and fundamental research, including TIFR and the atomic energy establishment.

questions.q: Homi Bhabha studied at the University of Cambridge in which country?
questions.explanation: Bhabha pursued his higher education and doctorate at the University of Cambridge in England.

questions.q: In what year did Homi Bhabha found the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research?
questions.explanation: TIFR was established in 1945, with support from the Sir Dorabji Tata Trust.

questions.q: Which family or trust provided crucial early funding to help Bhabha establish TIFR?
questions.explanation: The Sir Dorabji Tata Trust gave the founding support for TIFR, reflected in the institute's name.

questions.q: Bhabha became the first chairman of which Indian government body?
options: The Atomic Energy Commission of India
questions.explanation: Bhabha was the founding chairman of India's Atomic Energy Commission, set up in 1948.

questions.q: Apsara, commissioned in 1956 at Trombay under Bhabha's leadership, was notable as what?
options: The world's first fast breeder reactor
questions.explanation: Apsara was the first nuclear research reactor in Asia, commissioned at Trombay in 1956.

questions.q: In which year did Homi Bhabha die in an air crash?
questions.explanation: Bhabha died in 1966 when Air India Flight 101 crashed near Mont Blanc in the Alps.

questions.q: Bhabha died in the 1966 crash of an Air India flight near which European landmark?
questions.explanation: Air India Flight 101 crashed into Mont Blanc in the Alps, killing Bhabha and all aboard.

questions.q: Bhabha and W. Heitler developed an influential theory describing what phenomenon in cosmic rays?
questions.explanation: The Bhabha-Heitler theory explained the cascade (shower) production of electrons and positrons by cosmic rays.

questions.q: Which prestigious British scientific society elected Homi Bhabha a Fellow in 1941?
questions.explanation: Bhabha was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) in 1941 for his contributions to physics.

questions.q: Because India had abundant reserves of one element, Bhabha championed a three-stage nuclear power plan centred on it. Which element?
questions.explanation: Bhabha designed a three-stage programme to exploit India's vast thorium reserves for long-term nuclear power.

questions.q: Which high civilian honour did the Government of India award Homi Bhabha in 1954?
questions.explanation: Bhabha received the Padma Bhushan in 1954, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: Bhabha's earliest doctoral and research work in the 1930s was heavily focused on which area?
questions.explanation: His Cambridge research centred on cosmic rays and the physics of high-energy particles.

questions.q: The atomic energy establishment Bhabha built at Trombay was originally known by what name before being renamed BARC?
questions.explanation: It was first called the Atomic Energy Establishment, Trombay (AEET), and renamed BARC in 1967 after his death.

questions.q: Bhabha earned his doctorate at Cambridge under the influence of which renowned physicist who supervised much of his cosmic-ray work?
questions.explanation: Bhabha completed his PhD at Cambridge with Ralph H. Fowler as his doctoral adviser.

questions.q: The flight number of the Air India plane that crashed near Mont Blanc, killing Bhabha in 1966, was which of the following?
questions.explanation: Bhabha died aboard Air India Flight 101, the 'Kanchenjunga', which crashed on Mont Blanc in January 1966.

questions.q: Before TIFR, Bhabha spent the early 1940s conducting research at which Indian institution, where C. V. Raman had invited him?
questions.explanation: Bhabha worked at the Indian Institute of Science in Bangalore, where Raman headed the physics department.

questions.q: Bhabha presided over which major international conference in Geneva in 1955?
options: The First UN Conference on the Peaceful Uses of Atomic Energy
questions.explanation: Bhabha served as president of the first UN International Conference on the Peaceful Uses of Atomic Energy in Geneva in 1955.

questions.q: In a 1937 paper with Heitler, Bhabha explained the variation of cosmic-ray intensity with altitude, helping confirm the existence of which particle?
questions.explanation: Their cascade theory helped account for penetrating cosmic-ray particles, supporting the identification of the muon (mesotron).

questions.q: Besides science, Homi Bhabha was an accomplished practitioner of which of the arts?
questions.explanation: Bhabha was a talented painter and sketch artist, and a patron of the arts who decorated TIFR with artworks.

questions.q: Bhabha's calculation of the cross-section for electron-positron scattering was framed within which theory of physics?
questions.explanation: Bhabha scattering is a quantum electrodynamics (QED) process, and Bhabha derived its cross-section using relativistic quantum theory.

questions.q: The Homi Bhabha Fellowship and the Homi Bhabha National Institute were established to honour his contributions to which sector in India?
options: Nuclear science and higher research education
questions.explanation: These institutions honour Bhabha's legacy in advancing nuclear science and advanced research education in India.
